잠언 26:1.content: Like snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.

잠언 26:2.content: Like a fluttering sparrow, like a darting swallow, so the undeserved curse doesn’t come to rest.

잠언 26:3.content: A whip is for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the back of fools!

잠언 26:4.content: Don’t answer a fool according to his folly, lest you also be like him.

잠언 26:5.content: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.

잠언 26:6.content: One who sends a message by the hand of a fool is cutting off feet and drinking violence.

잠언 26:7.content: Like the legs of the lame that hang loose, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

잠언 26:8.content: As one who binds a stone in a sling, so is he who gives honor to a fool.

잠언 26:9.content: Like a thorn bush that goes into the hand of a drunkard, so is a parable in the mouth of fools.

잠언 26:10.content: As an archer who wounds all, so is he who hires a fool or he who hires those who pass by.

잠언 26:11.content: As a dog that returns to his vomit, so is a fool who repeats his folly.

잠언 26:12.content: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.

잠언 26:13.content: The sluggard says, “There is a lion in the road! A fierce lion roams the streets!”

잠언 26:14.content: As the door turns on its hinges, so does the sluggard on his bed.

잠언 26:15.content: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish. He is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth.

잠언 26:16.content: The sluggard is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who answer with discretion.

잠언 26:17.content: Like one who grabs a dog’s ears is one who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own.

잠언 26:18.content: Like a madman who shoots torches, arrows, and death,

잠언 26:19.content: is the man who deceives his neighbor and says, “Am I not joking?”

잠언 26:20.content: For lack of wood a fire goes out. Without gossip, a quarrel dies down.

잠언 26:21.content: As coals are to hot embers, and wood to fire, so is a contentious man to kindling strife.

잠언 26:22.content: The words of a whisperer are as dainty morsels, they go down into the innermost parts.

잠언 26:23.content: Like silver dross on an earthen vessel are the lips of a fervent one with an evil heart.

잠언 26:24.content: A malicious man disguises himself with his lips, but he harbors evil in his heart.

잠언 26:25.content: When his speech is charming, don’t believe him, for there are seven abominations in his heart.

잠언 26:26.content: His malice may be concealed by deception, but his wickedness will be exposed in the assembly.

잠언 26:27.content: Whoever digs a pit shall fall into it. Whoever rolls a stone, it will come back on him.

잠언 26:28.content: A lying tongue hates those it hurts; and a flattering mouth works ruin.
